text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

EXAMINATION: Computed tomography of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with intravenous contrast

INDICATION: 63-year-old female with known metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, status post right cytoreductive nephrectomy. Currently on cabozantinib 40 mg daily (initiated approximately 3 months ago following disease progression on Opdivo monotherapy). Restaging evaluation.

COMPARISON: Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 3 months earlier (at time of cabozantinib initiation/disease progression on Opdivo), and original baseline CT from approximately 9 months prior.

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT acquisition of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of intravenous iohexol contrast. Images obtained in the portal venous phase. Axial, coronal, and sagittal reformats reviewed. Lung window, soft tissue, and bone window reconstructions reviewed.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The dominant right lower lobe pulmonary nodule, previously measuring 2.6 cm at the most recent prior scan, now measures approximately 2.3 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 2.6 cm). This nodule demonstrates internal heterogeneity with a central hypodense zone suggestive of necrosis or treatment effect. The margins remain lobulated. There is a slight interval decrease in size compared to the prior examination. Two additional right lower lobe nodules are also present: the more anterior of these, previously 0.5 cm, appears grossly stable at approximately 0.5 cm; a second right lower lobe nodule, previously 0.5 cm, appears minimally decreased or stable at approximately 0.4 to 0.5 cm.

Two nodules identified in the right middle lobe at the prior examination — previously measuring 0.5 cm and 0.3 cm — appear slightly decreased or stable, now measuring approximately 0.4 cm and 0.3 cm, respectively. These remain well-circumscribed and show no evidence of growth.

Left lower lobe: The previously identified left lower lobe nodule, which has been slowly responding since cabozantinib initiation, now measures approximately 0.4 cm (previously 0.4 cm), grossly stable.

No new pulmonary nodules are identified. No consolidation. No ground-glass opacity or pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. The central airways are patent. No endobronchial lesions.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. No pathologic mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. No pericardial effusion. Heart is normal in size. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. No anterior chest wall lesions.

Pleural Spaces: No pleural effusion. No pleural nodularity.

Soft tissues / Chest wall: Unremarkable.

---

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Nephrectomy Bed / Upper Abdomen: Status post right radical nephrectomy. The right renal fossa demonstrates expected postsurgical changes with fat stranding and soft tissue remodeling. No discrete mass or nodularity in the nephrectomy bed to suggest local recurrence. No perihepatic or perisplenic fluid.

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth margins. No focal hepatic lesions. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. The portal vein is patent.

Gallbladder and Biliary: Gallbladder is present, with no cholelithiasis or wall thickening. The common bile duct is within normal limits.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Pancreas visualized to the tail with no focal mass or ductal dilatation.

Left Kidney and Adrenal Glands: The solitary left kidney appears mildly hypertrophied, consistent with compensatory change following right nephrectomy. No hydronephrosis. No renal calculi. No perinephric stranding. Left adrenal gland is normal in appearance. The right adrenal gland was previously resected en bloc; no recurrent adrenal tissue or mass identified in the right adrenal bed.

Retroperitoneum and Lymph Nodes: The right paracaval lymph node previously measuring 1.1 cm at the time of disease progression on nivo, and 0.7 cm at the most recent prior scan, now measures approximately 0.5 to 0.6 cm in short axis. This continued decrease in size is consistent with an ongoing treatment response. No new retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. No aortocaval or para-aortic lymph nodes of pathologic size are identified.

Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The right renal vein and infrarenal IVC are unremarkable. No thrombosis.

Small and Large Bowel: The visualized small and large bowel appear normal without evidence of wall thickening, obstruction, or pneumatosis. No free intraperitoneal air.

Pelvis: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. The bladder demonstrates normal wall thickness with no intraluminal filling defects. Uterus and adnexa appear grossly unremarkable for age. No free pelvic fluid.

Bones (visualized): No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions identified within the imaged skeleton. No pathologic fractures. Mild degenerative changes are noted in the lower lumbar spine, consistent with prior imaging.

Soft Tissues: No subcutaneous or soft tissue masses. No abdominal wall hernia.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Dominant right lower lobe pulmonary nodule demonstrates a slight interval decrease in size, now measuring approximately 2.3 cm compared to 2.6 cm on the prior examination. The internal morphology has changed slightly, with an evolving central hypodense region that may reflect treatment-related necrosis.

2. Remaining bilateral pulmonary nodules, including right lower lobe, right middle lobe, and left lower lobe lesions, are grossly stable to minimally decreased in size. No new pulmonary nodules.

3. Right paracaval lymph node continues to decrease, now measuring approximately 0.5 to 0.6 cm, consistent with ongoing treatment response.

4. No new sites of metastatic disease identified. No hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or intracranial metastases (intracranial disease not assessed on this examination).

5. Status post right radical nephrectomy with expected postoperative changes and no evidence of local recurrence.

6. Mild compensatory hypertrophy of the solitary left kidney without hydronephrosis or complicating features.

Overall, the findings are consistent with disease stability and a modest degree of treatment response across all sites of known metastatic disease in the setting of ongoing cabozantinib therapy.

---

Dictated and electronically signed by: Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging Section
